context.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?
context.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 16?

decision.primary_crux: The move from a prior fair-value center near 0.86 to a market price of 0.91 is best treated as repricing rather than a broken thesis: BTC is still more likely than not to close above $72,000 on the April 16 noon ET Binance minute, but the market is now too rich for a one-day, single-minute settlement with still-meaningful 3% downside and wick risk.
secondary_cruxes: The disagreement remains about exact-minute path risk, not contract wording or direction.
With only about 26 hours left the Yes case is stronger than for longer-dated thresholds, but not strong enough to justify paying above 0.90 from this evidence set.
No new bounded evidence materially reduces the late-path downside tail; the refresh mainly worsens the execution price.

valuation.compression_reason: No new bounded evidence justifies changing the prior probability range; the refresh adds price movement but not stronger verification of exact-minute stability or downside-tail suppression.

portfolio_constraints: Do not add fresh exposure at the updated market price.
Treat exact-minute Binance settlement risk as the dominant execution constraint even when spot remains above strike.
Reopen only if market cheapens materially or a fresher pre-fix Binance check materially improves confidence in the cushion and path.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: Would require a much cheaper price and still a fresh Binance check near settlement.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: Potential value zone only if same-venue cushion remains intact and volatility stays contained.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Bounded fair-value zone; maintain watch-only hold posture.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Above fair value for a one-minute settlement contract with meaningful path dependence.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Avoid or exit at very rich prices because exact-minute downside tail dominates residual uncertainty.

thesis_breakers: BTC loses cushion and trades materially back toward 72k-73k on Binance before the Apr 16 fixing window.
A concrete downside catalyst or volatility shock materially raises the odds of a sub-72k settlement minute.
Evidence appears that the operative Binance settlement presentation differs materially from the assumed minute mapping.
time_breakers: A fresh direct Binance observation closer to Apr 16 12:00 ET should supersede this packet before any action.
The actual Apr 16 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close fully resolves and obsoletes this judgment.
market_structure_breakers: Market price moves materially again without corresponding same-venue spot support, changing the value comparison.
Binance operational or microstructure issues emerge near settlement.
reversal_conditions: Upgrade only if price becomes materially cheaper relative to fair value or if a fresh pre-fix Binance check shows a meaningfully larger cushion.
Downgrade if BTC loses cushion or if downside volatility or catalyst risk increases.
Do not auto-reverse from this packet.

key_uncertainties: The true probability of a roughly 3% downside move or wick landing exactly at the settlement minute.
Whether the market embeds fresher venue-specific information than the bounded package contains.
How much the minor Binance chart/UI settlement-surface dependence matters in practice.
what_would_change_my_mind: A fresh Binance check near settlement showing sustained trade well above 72k with subdued volatility would move me somewhat closer to market.
A move back toward the low-73k or high-72k area would lower fair value materially.
A materially cheaper market price with unchanged Binance cushion would create a cleaner edge.
reasons_to_pass: The updated market price is above bounded fair value.
This is a one-minute single-venue settlement contract where path risk matters more than broad BTC direction.
The refresh added price movement, not new evidence supporting a higher probability.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: BTC is still more likely than not to finish above $72,000 on the April 16 Binance noon minute, but the market's move to 0.91 pushes price well above fair value centered near 0.86, so the disciplined output remains watch-only because exact-minute downside-path risk is still too important to ignore.
audit.notes: This refresh is primarily a valuation deterioration: same directional Yes, weaker execution case after repricing.
